# API Standards

> Generated by Velocity /init. Customize for your team.
> Referenced by: Architecture Agent, API Engineer subagent, guardrail checks.

## Versioning

- All public APIs must be versioned from day one: `/api/v1/`, `/api/v2/`.
- Breaking changes require a new major version.
- Deprecate old versions with a sunset date before removing.
- Never remove a version without communicating the migration path.

## Design

- Resource-oriented URLs for REST: `/payments/{id}`, not `/getPayment?id=`.
- HTTP verbs: GET (read), POST (create), PUT/PATCH (update), DELETE (remove).
- Use standard HTTP status codes consistently.
- All endpoints must return a consistent error response shape.

## Error Responses

Standard error shape:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "PAYMENT_DECLINED",
    "message": "The payment was declined by the card issuer.",
    "details": {},
    "trace_id": "abc123"
  }
}
```

- `code`: machine-readable, SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
- `message`: human-readable, actionable
- `trace_id`: correlates to logs for debugging

## Contracts

- Document all endpoints with OpenAPI/Swagger or GraphQL schema.
- Consumer-driven contract tests for service-to-service APIs.
- Schema changes require reviewer approval.

## Performance

- API response time target: p99 < 500ms for synchronous endpoints.
- For slow operations: respond with 202 Accepted and a status endpoint.
- Paginate list endpoints. Never return unbounded collections.

## Security

- All authenticated endpoints require a valid token.
- Rate limiting on all public endpoints.
- CORS configured explicitly — no wildcard `*` in production.

## Stack-Specific Notes

> Velocity /init will add stack-specific API guidance here (e.g., Spring MVC conventions, NestJS decorators, GraphQL resolver patterns).
